assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run Python and pip locally.
No Cohere account, trial API key, SDK install, or Chat request exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Cohere account, obtain a trial API key, and run a first native Python Chat call that prints a Command model response.

first_success.outcome: A Python ClientV2 Chat request returns and prints a Cohere message response.
first_success.observable_signal: The executed program prints response.message.content text from the Chat endpoint.

route_selection.surface: Cohere Platform dashboard signup plus native Python SDK quickstart
route_selection.rule: Prefer the Cohere Platform trial-key route over cloud private deployments; resolve email versus Google or GitHub signup only when public evidence shows one lowest-friction complete path.

candidates.name: Email and password signup on dashboard.cohere.com/welcome/register
candidates.reason: Public register UI exposes Email and Password plus Terms agreement, but email verification, captcha, and post-submit transitions are not documented.

candidates.name: Continue with Google or Continue with Github
candidates.reason: Peer OAuth buttons sit beside email signup; provider consent screens and whether they skip verification are not established from public evidence.

candidates.name: Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker, Azure, or private deployment clients
candidates.reason: Official installation docs present these as alternate platforms with different credentials and longer setup than the Cohere Platform trial key.

required_path.action: Create a Cohere account from the dashboard register surface.

required_fields.label: Email
required_fields.notes: Visible on the email signup form; OAuth routes may not collect this field directly.

required_fields.label: Password
required_fields.notes: Visible on the email signup form only.

required_fields.label: Terms of Use and Privacy Policy acknowledgement
required_fields.notes: Signup states that clicking Sign up constitutes agreement.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated Cohere account that can open the dashboard.

required_path.action: Open API Keys and copy the automatically issued Trial API key.
required_path.observable_result: A Trial API key value is available to paste into local code.

required_path.action: Install or upgrade the Cohere Python SDK.
required_path.observable_result: The cohere package is available in the local Python environment.

required_path.action: Create a ClientV2 with the trial key and call chat with a user message, then print the response text.
required_path.observable_result: The program prints Chat response text from a Command model.

prerequisites.description: Local Python and pip are available to install the Cohere SDK.

external_gates.description: Signup requires accepting Cohere Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.

platform_outcomes.description: Cohere automatically creates a Trial API key when an account is created.
The Chat endpoint returns generated message content for the submitted user prompt.

excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

excluded.item: Bedrock, SageMaker, Azure, or private-deployment client setup
excluded.reason: These are alternate platforms with different credentials than the Cohere Platform trial route.

sources.title: Register | Cohere
evidence_supported: Terms of Use and Privacy Policy agreement on Sign up

sources.title: Text generation - quickstart
evidence_supported: Chat call and printed response text

sources.title: Pricing FAQ: How do I get a Trial API key?
evidence_supported: Trial API key is created automatically on account creation
Key is available on the dashboard to copy

sources.title: Installation
evidence_supported: Cohere Platform versus private and cloud deployment options

uncertainties.question: Which signup identity route (email/password, Google, or Github) is the shortest complete path, and what verification or challenge steps follow Submit?
uncertainties.impact: The routes differ in external authorization handoffs and possibly email waits; field counts and action counts cannot be fixed until one route is chosen and walked.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough of each public register option showing post-submit screens, verification, and when the Trial key first appears.

uncertainties.question: Does any captcha, phone challenge, or email verification appear on the current email signup path?
uncertainties.impact: Hidden challenges would add required fields or an unavoidable wait not visible on the static register page.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Observed-public-ui or authenticated screenshots from a cold email registration attempt.
